title: centaurea degenianiformis
intro: Centaurea degenianiformis, also known as the Degen's knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Centaurea degenianiformis include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and soothe digestive issues. Centaurea degenianiformis can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine as a poultice or salve to apply topically to affected areas.
